notes: 16 seed cases. Per the build guide, expand this to 50-100 by hand over time, and add every real production failure you find as a new case with expected_difficulty noting why it's hard.

cases.input: Hello, I noticed I was billed $49 twice this month for the same subscription. Can you please refund the extra charge?
cases.expected_summary: Customer reports a duplicate charge and requests a refund.

cases.input: What's the difference between your Pro and Enterprise pricing plans? I'm trying to decide which one to upgrade to.
cases.expected_summary: Customer is comparing pricing plans before upgrading.

cases.input: The app keeps crashing every time I try to upload a photo. I've tried restarting my phone and it still happens.
cases.expected_summary: App crashes on photo upload even after restarting the device.

cases.input: I upgraded to the new plan but the analytics dashboard feature I paid for still shows 'locked'. Is this a bug?
cases.expected_summary: Paid feature is still showing as locked after upgrading.
cases.notes: Could be miscategorized as billing since it mentions a paid upgrade; the actual issue is access/functionality.

cases.input: I reset my password twice but it still says invalid credentials when I try to log in.
cases.expected_summary: Customer can't log in even after resetting their password.

cases.input: Can you update the email address on my account? I no longer have access to my old one.
cases.expected_summary: Customer wants to update their account email address.

cases.input: Hey team, just wanted to say your support has been amazing. Keep it up!
cases.expected_summary: Customer is giving positive feedback about support.

cases.input: Do you guys have an office in Mumbai?
cases.expected_summary: Customer is asking whether the company has a Mumbai office.

cases.input: My subscription payment failed last night and now I can't log into my account at all — is that related?
cases.expected_summary: A failed subscription payment appears to have locked the customer out of their account.
cases.notes: Genuinely spans billing and account. Labeled billing because the failed payment is the root cause, but account is a defensible answer too — good case to watch for flip-flopping across prompt versions.

cases.expected_summary: Customer is asking about getting a refund.
cases.notes: Extremely short, no context. Tests whether the model over-infers or handles minimal input gracefully.

cases.input: hii cant acces my acount its been 2 dyas since i mailed no relpy plz help fastt
cases.expected_summary: Customer cannot access their account and has not received a reply in two days.
cases.notes: Heavy typos and no punctuation — tests robustness to messy real-world input.

cases.input: Mera invoice galat hai, extra amount charge hua hai is month, please check and refund kardo.
cases.expected_summary: Customer says their invoice is incorrect with an extra charge and wants a refund.
cases.notes: Hindi-English code-mixed input. Common in real support inboxes for India-based businesses; tests whether the model handles it without misclassifying as 'general' due to unfamiliar phrasing.

cases.input: Oh great, my account got suspended for 'no reason' right before my big presentation. Love the timing. Can someone actually look into this?
cases.expected_summary: Customer's account was suspended and they are frustrated, requesting it be reviewed.
cases.notes: Sarcasm could confuse a model into tagging as 'general' or missing the actionable request underneath the tone.

cases.input: Every time I export a report to PDF, the file comes out completely blank. CSV export works fine though.
cases.expected_summary: PDF export produces blank files while CSV export works correctly.
cases.notes: Specific, detailed bug report — good for testing summary quality, not just category.

cases.input: I want to permanently delete my account and all my data. Please confirm once it's done.
cases.expected_summary: Customer requests permanent deletion of their account and data.
cases.notes: High-stakes account request; miscategorizing this has real consequences in production.

cases.input: Two things: first, I was overcharged $12 last month, and second, the mobile app won't let me change my notification settings. Can you help with both?
cases.expected_summary: Customer reports an overcharge and a separate app settings bug in one message.
cases.notes: Contains two distinct issues (billing + technical) in one email. Labeled billing since it's mentioned first, but flag this as a case where single-label classification is a real limitation worth discussing in interviews.
